① Signal
Signal
Play the over 5.5.2 units
The Read
✓Stuff — 30% whiff, splitter 36% putaway, 9.6 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
✓Command — 70% FPS, 1.8 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
⚠Matchup — TB K-rate runs -17% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #2/30 vs RHP); whiffs 19% on his splitter — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
✓Volume — 6.3 IP avg — pitches deep; clears 5.5 with ~5.2 IP at his 9.6 K/9 pace, so there's room to spare.
✓Projection — 7.1K vs 5.5 (+1.6K), σ1.6, CAP 68%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 5.5 alt line, not the 6.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: High-confidence edge — 68% model conviction at 7.1K vs 5.5 line.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~5.2 IP to hit 5.5 at his 9.6 K/9 — his 6.3 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓FS finishes counts (36% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓1.8 BB/9 — no free passes; he attacks and every PA is a K chance
Bet loses if
✗TB handles his splitter (19% whiff) — his primary out pitch gets neutralized
✗TB K-rate runs -17% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~1.1K

The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, splitter 33% putaway, 9.0 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
✓Command — 68% FPS, 2.0 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
→Matchup — PHI K-rate runs +3% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #21/30 vs RHP); whiffs 27% on his splitter — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 5.6 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.5 IP at his 9.0 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 6.0K vs 4.5 (+1.5K), σ2.1, CAP 68%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: High-confidence edge — 68% model conviction at 6.0K vs 4.5 line.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.5 IP to hit 4.5 at his 9.0 K/9 — his 5.6 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓PHI whiffs 27% on his splitter — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓FS finishes counts (33% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Start-to-start variance (σ2.1K) — the projection is a median, not a floor

① Signal

Play the over 5.5.2 units
The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, slider 34% putaway, 10.1 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
→Command — 62% FPS, 3.6 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
⚠Matchup — CLE K-rate runs -4% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #18/30 vs RHP); whiffs 36% on his slider — a mild headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 5.4 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~4.9 IP at his 10.1 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.2 × oppK 21.9 → −0.12K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.1K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
✓Projection — 7.0K vs 5.5 (+1.5K), σ2.2, CAP 66%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 5.5 alt line, not the 6.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: High-confidence edge — 66% model conviction at 7.0K vs 5.5 line.
Bet wins if
✓CLE whiffs 36% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SL finishes counts (34% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~4.9 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 5.4 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

① Signal
Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, splitter 35% putaway, 8.3 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
⚠Command — 3.9 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
✓Matchup — SD K-rate runs +6% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #25/30 vs RHP); whiffs 32% on his splitter — a mild tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
✓Volume — 6.0 IP avg — pitches deep; clears 4.5 with ~4.9 IP at his 8.3 K/9 pace, so there's room to spare.
✓Projection — 5.5K vs 4.5 (+1.0K), σ1.6, CAP 62%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 5.5K vs 4.5. 62% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.9 IP to hit 4.5 at his 8.3 K/9 — his 6.0 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓SD whiffs 32% on his splitter — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓FS finishes counts (35% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗3.9 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash

① Signal
Signal
Over 6.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 27% whiff, curveball 32% putaway, 10.1 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
✓Command — 67% FPS, 2.1 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
→Matchup — MIL K-rate runs +3% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #11/30 vs RHP); whiffs 31% on his curveball — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 6.0 IP avg — clears 6.5 with ~5.8 IP at his 10.1 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 7.5K vs 6.5 (+1.0K), σ2.3, CAP 60%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 6.5 alt line, not the 0.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 7.5K vs 6.5. 60% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓MIL whiffs 31% on his curveball — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CU finishes counts (32% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓2.1 BB/9 — no free passes; he attacks and every PA is a K chance
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.8 IP to reach 6.5, right at his 6.0 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

L5.5
116
① Signal
Signal
Lean under 5.5 — size down.0.5 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 21% whiff, curveball 25% putaway, 7.2 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
⚠Command — 3.8 BB/9 — walks stretch innings and PAs, which can feed late Ks against the under.
→Matchup — CHC K-rate runs +4% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #14/30 vs LHP); whiffs 33% on his curveball — roughly neutral K environment for his mix.
✓Volume — 5.2 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 4.5K vs 5.5 (-1.0K), σ1.5, CAP 68%, 10 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
→Line — best value at the 5.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Under edge present (4.5K vs 5.5), 68% confidence — lean only.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~6.9 IP to reach 5.5 — beyond his 5.2 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓7.2 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 5.5
✓21% whiff on a curveball-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗7.2 K/9 is enough to reach 5.5 in a long outing

The Read
→Stuff — 23% whiff, sweeper 29% putaway, 7.3 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 67% FPS, 3.4 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
→Matchup — LAD K-rate runs -3% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #5/30 vs RHP); whiffs 35% on his sweeper — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
⚠Volume / leash — 5 starts, 5.4 IP avg — injury-return leash is a hard cap on K opportunity. The model already uses the short IP, but an early hook is the main way this loses and a thin sample understates his true variance.
⚠Projection — 4.0K vs 3.5 (+0.5K), σ1.8, CAP 55%, 5 starts — edge is large but σ/CAP on a thin sample read optimistic.
→Line — best value at the 3.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 4.0K vs 3.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.3 IP to hit 3.5 at his 7.3 K/9 — his 5.4 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓LAD whiffs 35% on his sweeper — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SW finishes counts (29% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Injury-return leash — a hook before ~4.3 IP ends the over no matter how he's throwing

The Read
→Stuff — 26% whiff, curveball 31% putaway, 9.0 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
⚠Command — 4.3 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
⚠Matchup — KC K-rate runs -6% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #12/30 vs RHP); whiffs 24% on his curveball — a mild headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 6.0 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.5 IP at his 9.0 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.9 × oppK 21.5 → −0.83K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.8K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
⚠Projection — 4.8K vs 4.5 (+0.3K), σ2.9, CAP 52%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 4.8K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.5 IP to hit 4.5 at his 9.0 K/9 — his 6.0 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓CU finishes counts (31% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗4.3 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash
✗KC K-rate runs -6% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~0.3K

The Read
✓Stuff — 32% whiff, changeup 36% putaway, 9.4 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
→Command — 66% FPS, 3.0 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — SEA K-rate runs +7% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #23/30 vs RHP); whiffs 33% on his changeup — a mild tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 4.7 IP avg — short outings; clears 5.5 with ~5.3 IP at his 9.4 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 5.8K vs 5.5 (+0.3K), σ2.3, CAP 52%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.8K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓SEA whiffs 33% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (36% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓SEA K-rate runs +7% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.3K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.3 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 4.7 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

The Read
→Stuff — 25% whiff, changeup 32% putaway, 7.1 K/9 — average whiff; a K ceiling exists but isn't his identity.
→Command — 63% FPS, 2.5 BB/9 — average count control; neutral for the under.
✓Matchup — MIA K-rate runs -4% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #8/30 vs RHP); whiffs 37% on his changeup — a mild K-suppressing lineup for his arsenal; reinforces the under.
✓Volume — 6.1 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.2 × oppK 21.8 → −0.12K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.1K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
✓Projection — 4.0K vs 4.5 (-0.5K), σ2.2, CAP 40%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 4.0K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓7.1 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 4.5
✓MIA K-rate runs -4% vs neutral for his mix — a naturally K-suppressing lineup
✓6.1 IP avg — modest length caps his total K opportunity
Bet loses if
✗7.1 K/9 is enough to reach 4.5 in a long outing

The Read
✓Stuff — 13% whiff, cutter 16% putaway, 5.3 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
→Command — 60% FPS, 2.4 BB/9 — average count control; neutral for the under.
⚠Matchup — CIN K-rate runs +19% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #30/30 vs RHP); whiffs 18% on his cutter — a strong tailwind; this lineup could push him toward the number.
✓Volume — 5.3 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 2.9K vs 3.5 (-0.6K), σ1.4, CAP 36%, 7 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
Verdict: Model projects 2.9K vs 3.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~5.9 IP to reach 3.5 — beyond his 5.3 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓5.3 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 3.5
✓13% whiff on a cutter-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗CIN K-rate runs +19% vs neutral for his mix — swing-happy enough to feed cheap Ks

The Read
✓Stuff — 15% whiff, curveball 18% putaway, 5.0 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
✓Command — 66% FPS, 1.9 BB/9 — pounds the zone early; batters put it in play before two-strike counts.
⚠Matchup — NYY K-rate runs +11% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #28/30 vs RHP); whiffs 35% on his curveball — a strong tailwind; this lineup could push him toward the number.
✓Volume — 4.8 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 2.8K vs 3.5 (-0.7K), σ0.6, CAP 32%, 8 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
Verdict: Model projects 2.8K vs 3.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~6.3 IP to reach 3.5 — beyond his 4.8 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓5.0 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 3.5
✓15% whiff on a curveball-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗NYY K-rate runs +11% vs neutral for his mix — swing-happy enough to feed cheap Ks

▼ UNDER · WATCH
CAP 28% · needs 46.3% (116) · model 65% (-187) · +18.8% edge
L6.5
116
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
⚠Stuff — 28% whiff, splitter 34% putaway, 8.6 K/9 — he can miss bats, so the under leans on the line and matchup, not his stuff.
→Command — 61% FPS, 1.8 BB/9 — average count control; neutral for the under.
✓Matchup — AZ K-rate runs -15% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #4/30 vs RHP); whiffs 28% on his splitter — a strong K-suppressing lineup for his arsenal; reinforces the under.
⚠Volume — 6.7 IP avg — goes deep, the under's main risk: more PAs = more K chances (needs ~6.8 IP to reach 6.5).
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.4 × oppK 19.3 → −0.82K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.8K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
✓Projection — 4.9K vs 6.5 (-1.6K), σ2.4, CAP 28%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 6.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 4.9K vs 6.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~6.8 IP to reach 6.5 — beyond his 6.7 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓AZ K-rate runs -15% vs neutral for his mix — a naturally K-suppressing lineup
Bet loses if
✗8.6 K/9 — the rate exists to reach 6.5 on a deep outing or if the lineup expands the zone
✗28% whiff — if he's sharp, Ks stack even against a contact lineup
✗6.7 IP avg — deep outings hand him extra PAs to climb toward 6.5

✓Command — 68% FPS, 2.8 BB/9 — pounds the zone early; batters put it in play before two-strike counts.
⚠Matchup — PIT K-rate runs +14% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #29/30 vs LHP); whiffs 32% on his slider — a strong tailwind; this lineup could push him toward the number.
✓Volume — 4.9 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 5.0K vs 5.5 (-0.5K), σ1.9, CAP 38%, 8 starts — clean edge at current pricing.

→Line — best value at the 3.5 alt line, not the 5.0 market main — be on the right number.

The Read
→Stuff — 23% whiff, slider 28% putaway, 8.6 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 64% FPS, 2.7 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — CWS K-rate runs +6% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #26/30 vs LHP); whiffs 34% on his slider — a mild tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 5.4 IP avg — short outings; clears 5.5 with ~5.8 IP at his 8.6 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 5.7K vs 5.5 (+0.2K), σ2.3, CAP 50%, 8 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.7K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

The Read
✓Stuff — 28% whiff, four-seam fastball 33% putaway, 10.5 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
✓Command — 64% FPS, 1.7 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
⚠Matchup — WSH K-rate runs -13% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #6/30 vs RHP); whiffs 18% on his four-seam fastball — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 5.9 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~4.7 IP at his 10.5 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.6 × oppK 19.8 → −1.27K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 1.3K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
⚠Projection — 5.6K vs 5.5 (+0.1K), σ2.6, CAP 49%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
Verdict: Model projects 5.6K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

→Volume — 5.2 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.6 IP at his 8.8 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.

→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.

→Stuff — 23% whiff, curveball 26% putaway, 8.4 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 62% FPS, 3.6 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
⚠Matchup — ATL K-rate runs -6% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #8/30 vs LHP); whiffs 33% on his curveball — a mild headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
⚠Volume — 4.6 IP avg — short outings; clears 4.5 with ~4.8 IP at his 8.4 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.8 × oppK 21.8 → −0.59K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.6K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
⚠Projection — 4.3K vs 4.5 (-0.2K), σ2.8, CAP 44%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.0 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Model projects 4.3K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

The Read
✓Stuff — 26% whiff, curveball 32% putaway, 8.8 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
→Command — 60% FPS, 3.5 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — BOS K-rate runs +10% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #20/30 vs RHP); whiffs 29% on his curveball — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 5.1 IP avg — short outings; clears 5.5 with ~5.6 IP at his 8.8 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 5.4K vs 5.5 (-0.1K), σ1.9, CAP 45%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.4K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

MODEL
signals · methodology · card guide · v9.9

■ Win rate bar fills to actual %▲ Bias positive = model tends to over-project Ks◆ MAE in strikeouts — lower is better● Bias within range
Signal Tiers — What Each Badge Means
STRONG · 2uHighest conviction. Model projects meaningfully more Ks than the line with strong probability and high confidence across multiple signals. Recommended 2 unit bet.
MOD · 1uModerate conviction. Clear edge vs the line with reasonable confidence. Standard 1 unit bet (1.5u when confidence is elevated and variance is low).
LEAN O · 0.5uDirectional lean over. Model sees an edge but confidence or probability does not clear the full action threshold. Half unit only.
LEAN U · 0.5uDirectional lean under. Model projects below the line with some confidence but not enough for full conviction. Half unit only.
UNDER · 1uHigh conviction fade. Model projects meaningfully fewer Ks than the line with strong confidence. 1 unit bet (1.5u when confidence is highest).
WATCH ⇑⇓Signal exists but does not clear the action threshold — confidence too low or variance too high. No bet recommended. Orange = over direction, Silver = under. Blocker reason shown on the card.
PASSNo meaningful signal. Model projects close to the line or insufficient data to form a view.
Overlay Flags
LEASH — injury-return / short-leash arm; the over tier is capped one notch (direction holds, size comes down) until he shows 6+ IP
FADE / K-SUPP — contact-oriented lineup that suppresses strikeouts vs this arsenal — caution on overs
HIGH-σ — volatile start-to-start K totals; the projection is a median, not a floor. On its own this is fine — it only bites when paired with a contact lineup (see σ-FADE)
σ-FADE — the volatile-arm-vs-contact-lineup combo. Historically this exact pairing over-projects strikeouts, so the model now trims the projection on a sliding scale (bigger trim the more volatile the arm AND the more contact-oriented the lineup)
THIN — small sample (<8 starts); confidence lower than the number implies
ALT — best value is on an alternate line, not the market main — be on the right number
These are recorded with each bet and shown in the Results tab, so past performance can be sliced by flag.
CAP — Model Confidence

CAP (0-100%) measures how aligned the signals are — not a win probability. High CAP means multiple independent signals point the same direction. Used to gate tier assignment — higher tiers require higher CAP.

P(win) and the Price You Need
Every card shows three probabilities, and they are not the same thing.

Pois — a textbook strikeout model. It assumes a pitcher is as steady as a coin flip, so it is a little too optimistic.
Sim — the same idea but allowing for the fact that real pitchers are streaky. Always a bit lower than Pois, and closer to the truth.
P(win) — the one that matters. We take Sim and correct it against every bet this model has actually graded, so it reflects how often bets like this one really land. It is deliberately humble: models are almost always overconfident, so P(win) is pulled down toward what the track record supports.

BE / NEED — P(win) converted into a price. If a bet wins 60% of the time, it is only worth taking at −150 or better; at −200 you would need to win 67%, so you would lose money slowly even while winning most bets. The NEED number is that break-even price, and it is exactly what P(win) implies — no hidden padding.

How to use it: compare NEED to the price you can actually get. FanDuel is shown only as a reference; if you shop and beat NEED, the bet is live. If you cannot get there, pass — a good read at a bad price is still a bad bet.

Note: CAP is a conviction score used to decide which bets qualify. It is not a win probability — only P(win) is.
Reading a Card
The Read — bulleted matchup analysis: each line is a data point → what it means → direction (✓ supports · ⚠ risk · → neutral), ending in a Verdict tied to sizing
PROJ strip — model projection, edge vs line, CAP, and NEED: the worst price at which this bet is still profitable. Green = the FD reference price already clears it; amber = you need to shop for better.
OPP K-MIX strip — three K rates on one comparable scale: BBRef season team K% · MIX-K% (re-weighted for this pitcher's arsenal — can exceed BBRef when they're extra vulnerable to his mix) · Blended (feeds the projection). Green = pitcher-favorable
Arsenal box — per-pitch usage, pitcher whiff%, opponent whiff%, matchup grade. Below it: STUFF (usage-weighted whiff), VS LINEUP (matchup amplifier bar — right/green = lineup amplifies his whiffs), K ENGINE (donut showing which pitches drive his strikeouts vs this lineup), and a recent hit-rate row (dot per last-10 start: green = went over, red = stayed under, with a side-aware tally)
Lineup panel — confirmed order; per batter, per pitch: whiff% (bold) over K% (lighter shade). Green = pitcher advantage, red = contact
Implied Odds box — probability of clearing each alt line as American odds. BET = recommended line, MKT = market main
Signal strip — action, unit size, and verdict
Data Sources
Live each session — pitcher L10 K counts, IP, ERA · FanDuel alt lines and odds · confirmed lineups
Savant CSV — batter K%/whiff%/xBA per pitch type · pitcher arsenal whiff%/FPS%/putaway%
Researched — team wRC+ by handedness · L10 runs/game · park factors · bullpen ERA · BBRef team K% splits
Not included — live Statcast splits · bullpen usage · real-time injury status beyond confirmed lineup
Hits TabBETA

P(≥1 hit) for each batter tonight. Built from confirmed lineups and pitcher arsenal data. Sorted by edge vs FanDuel's posted hit line. Early stage — no bullpen data or recent trends. Large edges often reflect model limitations, not market errors.

⚠ For informational and entertainment purposes only. Projections are model outputs — not guarantees. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Bet responsibly.
